title: Inline Mermaid Rendering Delivery
summary: Delivery tracker for local-only browser rendering of inline Mermaid fences with a pinned browser asset, session renderer, contained fallback, and Studio proof.
content_html:
Inline Mermaid Rendering Delivery

Outcome

A document in a repo-backed local scope renders fenced Mermaid after its generated HTML is mounted. The canonical source remains inline Markdown. Public and local_external scopes continue to receive only the existing static SVG media workflow.

The concept and dependency decisions are settled. This is an implementation-ready tracker, but work remains bounded by its checkpoints and begins at IMD-0 only when implementation is authorized.

Delivery Blast Radius

area likely owner or evidence expected change
Markdown output studio/shared/python/markdown_renderer.py preserve the existing language-mermaid code class; add a regression contract rather than a server-side transform
Scope eligibility generated browser scope config and docs-viewer-config-controller.js use the active record's existing scopeType; every configured local scope is eligible without a feature flag or Studio special case
Document mount site/docs-viewer/runtime/js/shared/docs-viewer-document-controller.js invoke one post-mount inline-diagram adapter with explicit scope context
Browser dependency docs-viewer/build/mermaid/package.json, lockfile, and one feature-local sync command pin direct mermaid and CLI to 11.16.0; copy the upstream minified distribution and license to one tracked versioned runtime location
Browser renderer new focused shared Docs Viewer module plus the repository-versioned browser asset retain one loader/initialization promise per application session; render each mount sequentially with unique identities and stale-mount protection
Diagram host focused inline adapter and Diagram Detail View emit an adapter-owned inline-SVG host that the sibling feature can decorate later; do not add its control here
Styling shared Docs Viewer content CSS responsive diagram host, loading state, and visible error/fallback treatment
Static Mermaid build existing CLI producer and .mmd to SVG pipeline no behavior change
Static HTML export docs-viewer/services/docs_static_html_export.py no conversion or bundle addition; prove the inline code block is retained
Source-faithful exports Docs Viewer document export transforms and package contracts no conversion; prove canonical Markdown remains byte-faithful
Embedded content sub-scope reports, review/detail surfaces, and other content_html mounts excluded initially unless the host supplies the content scope's explicit scope_type
Verification focused Python/browser-module tests and one Studio proof document scope gate, session loading, navigation remount, sequential multiple diagrams, stale results, failure containment, and export preservation

Settled Dependency Contract

docs-viewer/build/mermaid/package.json owns two exact direct development dependencies:

"@mermaid-js/mermaid-cli": "11.16.0",
"mermaid": "11.16.0"

The adjacent lockfile remains the resolved dependency authority. A feature-local sync-browser-asset npm script verifies the direct Mermaid package version, then copies:

node_modules/mermaid/dist/mermaid.min.js
node_modules/mermaid/LICENSE

to checked output beneath:

site/docs-viewer/runtime/vendor/mermaid/11.16.0/

The update command is:

npm --prefix docs-viewer/build/mermaid run sync-browser-asset

The tracked runtime copy is generated output and is not edited by hand. Reader code lazy-loads only that versioned asset and never imports from node_modules, a CDN, the CLI, Puppeteer, or Node. IMD-0 measures the current approximately 3.4 MB minified asset in the local reader. If its transfer or parse cost is unacceptable, stop and revise this delivery around one small Mermaid-only bundle; do not introduce a general frontend bundler solely for this feature.

Delivery Tracker

Current state: proposed; product, dependency, lifecycle, fallback, and scope decisions are settled.

Next checkpoint: IMD-0 only. Do not advance to the renderer before its dependency, mount, and public-exclusion evidence is accepted.

Delivery Checkpoints

IMD-0 — Contract And Dependency Fit

  • [ ] Add direct exact mermaid 11.16.0 beside the exact CLI dependency and update the existing lockfile without a second version.
  • [ ] Add the feature-local sync command, checked minified runtime asset, and required license at the settled versioned location.
  • [ ] Measure first lazy load and parse in the local reader; accept the checked upstream asset or stop for a Mermaid-only bundling revision.
  • [ ] Confirm the shared Markdown renderer still emits <pre><code class="language-mermaid"> without a server-side transform.
  • [ ] Confirm the selected content scope's existing scopeType is available immediately after the ordinary document mount.
  • [ ] Prove public and external-local routes neither request nor initialize the browser renderer.
  • [ ] Define the adapter-owned diagram host so the sibling detail-view delivery can decorate it later without implementing that control now.
  • [ ] Stop if the feature requires a general frontend bundler or broad runtime/build-system refactor.

IMD-1 — Focused Renderer

  • [ ] Add one lazy inline-Mermaid adapter with a module-level loader promise and one strict initialization per application session.
  • [ ] After every eligible normal document mount, render its fences sequentially with unique render identities.
  • [ ] Guard every asynchronous result with the current mount generation so navigation cannot insert stale SVG into replacement content.
  • [ ] On success, replace the fence with the adapter-owned responsive inline-SVG host and mark it against duplicate processing.
  • [ ] On failure, retain the original code block and show Diagram could not be rendered. Mermaid source is shown below. as a politely announced associated status.
  • [ ] Keep detailed exceptions in the developer console; add no stack, large parser response, Retry control, or editor action to document content.
  • [ ] Contain one broken diagram without failing later fences or the document, and release per-mount state after navigation.

IMD-2 — Scope And Surface Boundary

  • [ ] Enable through the single rule that the active content scope is exactly local; add no per-scope feature flag or scopeId === "studio" branch.
  • [ ] Make every configured local scope eligible while loading no asset for a mounted document without Mermaid fences.
  • [ ] Use one useful Studio inline diagram as the only initial proof content and acceptance surface; do not add or alter Processing content for proof.
  • [ ] Prove public and local_external fences remain ordinary code blocks and load no Mermaid asset.
  • [ ] Keep embedded report/review/detail mounts excluded unless their content scope context is explicit and tested.

IMD-3 — Export Regression And Closeout

  • [ ] Prove source-faithful package exports retain exact inline Markdown.
  • [ ] Prove static HTML export retains the generated code block without SVG generation or a Mermaid bundle.
  • [ ] Verify several diagrams, one invalid diagram, navigation away during rendering, repeated mounts, and session-level renderer reuse.
  • [ ] Check responsive layout, light/dark themes, keyboard/reading order, the visible fallback, and console-only diagnostics manually.
  • [ ] Update the durable Document Diagrams and Export owners with shipped behavior.
  • [ ] Close only after the focused runtime, public-exclusion, export, and manual browser evidence passes.

Explicitly Deferred

  • pre-rendering inline fences during static HTML export;
  • choosing rendered-versus-inline behavior per export profile;
  • public publication conversion;
  • automatic extraction to canonical .mmd and SVG;
  • inline editing widgets or live source-editor preview;
  • a general frontend bundler, application-wide asset pipeline, or independent browser Mermaid version;
  • per-scope inline-rendering flags, a Studio-only runtime branch, or Processing proof-content changes;
  • Retry controls, inline parser diagnostics, or source-edit actions in the failure state;
  • the sibling Diagram Detail View control or open-in-new-tab behavior;
  • rendering inside reports, review packages, or embedded detail documents without an explicit second delivery need.

The concept owns the product and export decisions. The shipped Mermaid Document Diagrams Delivery remains the implementation authority for separate .mmd source and published SVG.
